briefs.title: Disaster Financial Assistance Applications Now Require Upfront Documentation and Follow a New Approval Process
briefs.summary: BC updated its disaster financial assistance rules so businesses must now submit a full application — including invoices and supporting documents — right away, instead of just giving notice. The Provincial Emergency Program will review eligibility first, then send approved claims to an adjuster, who recommends a payout amount for final approval.
briefs.action_required: If you ever apply for disaster financial assistance, gather all invoices and documents before submitting your application.
